[h1]TL;DR: The game is a mess of broken, non-viable in-game mechanics and is dominated by bug abusing and exploits that the developers and incapable or unwilling to fix in a timely manner. So far the game in a dire state and there is no promise that it will improve any time soon. Hopefully the dev team will prove me wrong, but so far I cannot recommend this game even to casual audience due to broken matchmaking.[/h1]

[h3]A more detailed review below:[/h3]

The sad truth is that this game is simply unfinished. Rematch was promising and fun to play at the very beginning of its lifetime as nobody yet figured out how to break the game on every possible level. Right now the game is borderline unplayable for anyone who expects a balanced and deep experience of alternated Football rules that the developers initialy promised.

Gameplay integrity is compromised as every match is dominated by people who can exploit movement and dribbling mechanics to their fullest extent. Completely unreadable sudden spikes in movement that defy all laws of physics, bug abusing and exploiting of ball control mechanics to ignore inertia and momentum rules and incur heavy desyncs. 
Almost every possible way the developers intended for the game to work is non-functional and non-viable as players found more and more ways to break new barriers of what was possible with newly discovered exploits. 

All these issues compounded with the fact that matchmaking systems are utterly unreliable in their ability to create balanced matches in a reasonable amount of time (read, it usually takes 8-10 minutes to find a match [i]even in prime time of a highly populated region[/i], just to still play with people several ranks above yours).

By that point you might ask "but so what, the meta exists in every game, and people find optimal ways to win in any competitive experience, how's Rematch any different?". The difference is that in other competitive games, the meta naturally shapes up as players find creative ways of using INTENDED gameplay mechanics, not bugs or exploits that even developers acknowledge should not exist, but that is quite literally the ONLY way the game is played right now.

Here's the most sad part, the developers do agree that these exploits should not exist, so it would be all fine and dandy if they managed to switfly fix these problems and make the game actually function the way it was intended, but thats not the reality of the situation. 
Slo(w)clap, the developers, are [i]painfully[/i] slow to make any important decisions and they are so afraid to break something that every possible change is implemented in tiny, baby-steps that barely tip-top around the problems instead of fixing them outright. 

To be honest, this was almost too predictable of an outcome, because it all starts to make sense as soon as you realize that these are the developers of another infamous multiplayer game, ABSOLVER, that completely and utterly collapsed under the weight of all the exploits and balancing problems and inability of the dev team to adapt and rapidly fix those problems. 

The situation with Rematch is so staggeringly similar to what Absolver went through almost 9 years ago, that it becomes impossible to ignore just how little has changed with the developer and their practices in that time.

Jeu super réaliste. Rapide a prendre en main, difficile a maîtriser, un Gameplay nerveux et dynamique, une vibe foot de rue qui n'est pas sans déplaire.
On croise même des joueurs possédant un QI d'huître déshydraté, qui n'hésite pas a brailler dans leur micros une myriade d'insultes fleuris tout a fait représentative du public moyen du football. Qui comme la majorité des joueurs se prennent pour le nouveau Messi (ou CR7, choisissez votre camp, perso j'en ai rien a carré) et finissent invariablement par te faire perdre les parties, le tout en se faisant rattraper par la dure réalite: Le football est un jeu d'équipe.
Donc en ajoutant le gameplay au côté social du jeu, l'immersion dans le monde du Foot est totale et l'expérience est 100% réaliste, Je suis bluffé.
10/10

⚽ Rematch İncelemesi – Olumlu ve Olumsuz Yönler

Öncelikle, online futbol oyunu kıtlığı çektiğimiz bu dönemde böyle bir oyun geliştirdikleri için tüm ekibe teşekkür ederim. 🙏 Gerçekten keyifli saatler geçirdim. Ancak bazı sorunlar ne yazık ki ilk betadan beri çözülmedi ve artık oyuncu sayısı da ciddi şekilde düştü.

Eksiler 👎

⏳ Maç bulma süresi çok uzadı: Eskiden 3v3 hızlı maçlar için 1 dakika beklerken artık 5 dakikayı buluyor.

❌ Crossplay güncellemesi hayal kırıklığı: Bu ayarı kapattığımda bilgisayar oyuncularıyla eşleşemiyorum.

👶 Yeni oyuncu yoğunluğu: Sürekli oyunu bilmeyen veya tek başına oynamaya çalışan kişilerle eşleşiyorum. Takım oyunu neredeyse yok.

🤹 Bencil oyun tarzı: Pas vermek yerine çalım denemeleri arttı, oyun keyfi düşüyor.

🧤 Kaleci bugları: Üstüne gelen topu bir anda salma gibi hatalar var.

🐞 Bug abuse: Bazı oyuncular bug kullanarak çalım atıyor ve bu oldukça sinir bozucu.

Artılar 👍

🎮 Temel oynanış gerçekten eğlenceli.

👥 Arkadaşlarla girildiğinde keyif ikiye katlanıyor.

💸 Fiyat / Performans: Bana göre şu anki haliyle 15 dolara değmez. Ancak ben aldığım için pişman değilim çünkü güzel vakit geçirdim. Yeni başlayacaksanız biraz daha güncelleme gelmesini beklemenizi tavsiye ederim.
Arada girip 2 maç atılan çerezlik bir oyun.  Oyun içi konuşma olması gerekiyor ki pas atmayanlara sövebilelim.

I really enjoy this game, playing with my friends in quick play is fun for some casual gaming. Ranked is also mostly enjoyable. It's suffers from some of the same issues as other ranked multi-player games teammates often give up after conceding 1 or 2 goals leaving you a player short in a game that is usually winnable. I'd like to see some sort of system for protecting your RP when a teammate leaves early into a game. I also feel sometimes that I am being matched with players who are not the same skill level as I am when queuing ranked, there's no way to actually tell besides looking up a players name on a website that shows their stats. Despite this I have solo queued for low gold to elite and  have had a mostly great time doing so. A few issues that have to be addressed but regardless a fun game that I think was well worth the price

Seems like nobody cares about the game apart from the small playerbase it got left... Wayyy too rushed release, as per usual...

You feel stranded as a player. It doesn't matter what kind of news they put out, they never apologized for the lack of content and meaningful progression.

For the content it has in it it really should've been a free to play game, or at least early access. The only thing we had now was cosmetics and kind of fun gameplay with netcode that destroys the whole experience once you have fun.

The patches also feel like they're made by a single person. We haven't seen ANYTHING meaningful apart from the crossplay addition (that should have been there from the start - but again - rushed release).

There has been blatant bug using that could've been scripted but the devs just let it happen and only patch it THREE MONTHS+ into the games life cycle.

This is just a disappointment disguised as a fun football game... It's fun - until you see people lagging and winning because of it. And it's fun until you play a few games and wonder what the f* point is in paying for an unfinished game with 0 meaningful progression and literally only the gameplay being there.

I don't know who's fault this was but I'm pretty sure that the devs were as per just doing what they could. If this is another one of the management f* ups, print out this or another review and send it to the responsible *diot. Make them feel bad - they **ssed onto this studios reputation.

EDIT: It's still dog a month and a half later :)

EDIT EDIT: We still haven't seen any meaningful content or modes added. The game literally stayed the same over this whole time. Whoever is responsible for this mess should feel bad. They should stomp onto a sharp lego brick.

LAST EDIT: So I gave the game the chance until I reached the end part of level 69 with a fresh season and "new moves" apparently... I've seen those new moves like three times maybe and the only "good" cosmetics I've seen released since the game came out is a christmas sweater with a christmas hat. This should tell you how lack luster this game is and has been for the longest time. At release it felt like an early access game that was about to get fleshed out with real content creator and streamer momentum. And now everything that is left of the fun is that one round you get per game session that feels good. Getting that needs an investment of an hour plus to have 5 minutes of fun. All the other rounds are just plagued by sub par team mates that ball chase the living hell out of the game and play solo.

I uninstalled the game now and was even close to just removing it from my Steam library out of fear I would touch it again. But it's safe to say that Sloclap and Kepler have released a half baked game with at first solid gameplay and left it out to dry and never came back with anything meaningful. Sorry devs, but this one is one to forget.

I’ve written four reviews in the 11 years I’ve been on Steam. I’ve never felt this simultaneously annoyed and passionate about a game.

For a $30 title, this game has serious issues.

[strike]Why am I able to play ranked matches without a full team? Even in the new 3v3 mode, I’ve loaded into matches missing one, or even both, teammates. If there aren’t enough players to fill a lobby, put everyone back in queue until there are. I’d honestly take AI teammates over spending an entire match playing “chase the ball” just to lose MMR.[/strike]
Addressed in 1.201.002.

Why am I, a mid-Silver player, consistently matched against players in the upper tiers of ranked?

Why do I clearly have the ball at my feet, or in my hands, only for the game to decide it was actually ten feet behind me, sometimes already in the goal?

Why can I cleanly tackle someone, take the ball, and then watch them instantly reappear downfield with possession as if nothing happened?

Why is playing goalie less than a 50/50? You’re not just guessing which way to dive. You’re also gambling on whether the game decides to let you grab the ball at all.

I play in my own region, on my own servers, and still run into constant issues with core mechanics that make the game feel fundamentally unreliable. And beyond the gameplay itself, there’s also:

Teammates who won’t even finish a casual match without rage-quitting

Toxic teammates

A ranked system that barely rewards wins and heavily punishes losses at low ranks

No way to report or remove teammates who troll, own-goal, or camp goalie so you can’t save

Overpriced cosmetics

No ability to queue for multiple modes or match types

[strike]Forced 5v5 ranked[/strike]
S1 fixes this.

I loved this game when I first picked it up. It’s becoming harder and harder to keep playing with so many flaws in the core experience. I’m writing this review for the sake of the game’s future and for players like me who actually read Steam reviews before buying.

Fix the game, Sloclap, and I’ll fix my review. Win-win.

È finita. Non c’è altro modo per dirlo: Rematch è un gioco morto. E la colpa non è solo degli sviluppatori che hanno abbandonato il supporto tecnico per concentrarsi sulle skin, ma di una community che è diventata un cancro terminale per il titolo stesso. Gioco dal primo giorno, ho visto l’evoluzione di questo progetto e oggi mi trovo davanti a un deserto di tossicità e frustrazione che non ha eguali nel panorama dei giochi sportivi.

La tossicità qui non è più un "caso isolato", è la norma. È un ambiente pesante, asfissiante, dove la coordinazione di squadra è stata sostituita da un narcisismo becero: gente che non passa la palla nemmeno sotto tortura, insulti costanti in chat vocale e testuale, e un sistema di "quick chat" usato esclusivamente per bullizzare i propri compagni di squadra al minimo errore. Ma la cosa più paradossale e ridicola accade fuori dal campo, proprio qui su Steam. Guardate i commenti alle recensioni negative o ai post di chi segnala la tossicità: vengono puntualmente aggrediti, insultati e ridicolizzati dagli stessi elementi che stanno affossando il gioco. Se provi a dire che l'ambiente è invivibile, ti rispondono "skill issue" o ti augurano il peggio. È un cortocircuito logico che la dice lunga sul perché i numeri di Rematch siano crollati a picco.

La gente normale, quella che vuole farsi una partita per rilassarsi, è scappata. Sono rimasti solo i "tossici" che si alimentano del loro stesso veleno in server sempre più vuoti. I developer hanno fallito nel proteggere la playerbase sana e ora si ritrovano con un gioco fantasma dove il matchmaking dura un'eternità solo per metterti in una partita già persa con gente che ti insulta dal primo secondo. Il netcode è ancora rotto, i tackle passano attraverso i giocatori, i server laggano in modo imbarazzante, ma nulla di tutto ciò conta più quando l'anima del gioco è marcita.

Non sprecherò un secondo di più a discutere con chi verrà qui sotto a difendere l’indifendibile o a spargere altro odio. Proprio perché questo spazio è diventato lo specchio della discarica che è il gioco, disattiverò i commenti a questa recensione. Non c’è nulla da discutere con una community che si insulta da sola mentre affoga. Se state pensando di comprarlo o installarlo: non fatelo. Risparmiatevi i soldi e, soprattutto, la salute mentale. Rematch è il perfetto esempio di come una bella idea possa essere distrutta dalla peggiore utenza possibile

Has potential, but is unfortunately plagued by a bunch of strange bugs that make the game feel very unsatisfying to play. Unlike Rocket League your character will automatically "stick" to the ball to interact with it, which can cause some super janky gameplay when you have multiple players trying to lock onto the ball at the same time, or going for a save as a goal tender while another player somehow clips the ball through your hands and scores.

The game has pretty bad net-code and overall doesn't feel super great to play, the higher ranks are plagued with people abusing exploits and cheats to increase player speed, etc. Unfortunately you can tell that this game was designed by developers who were unfamiliar with Multiplayer as there's a lot of things that are tied to client-side and not server-side.

I want to like this game and have fun with it, but in it's current state it's more tiring to play than it is fun to play.

So this game is pretty fun but I have some issues with it.
First of all, its still pretty buggy, I've played since Beta and its gotten better but still buggy. Serves are laggy, games will randomly crash for both myself or teammates and then we won't be able to reconnect. Not only that, but people will rage quit if they don't get the ball and refuse to defend. The issue is when they rage quit, we don't get another player joining to replace the player that rage quit AND we still LOST POINTS IN RANKED FOR THIS. wtf. I've reached promotion for plat to diamond, 3 times now and all 3 times this has happened to me. It has taken me 4 different times to reach diamond. Not because of my skill, or my teammates skills, but because other players will troll and rage quit or they will DC randomly and we wont get another player in to replace them. That is my biggest issue with the game right now. Punish players who rage quit ESPECIALLY if one of their teammates is playing the promo series to go up in rank AND make it so another player joins the match if someone leaves or DC's.
Overall rate it 3/5 stars.

Here's my honest review having played this game on both PC and the PS5. This game is in a horrendous state at after almost every patch that goes out. While Sloclap has a general idea of what they want to do with Rematch it's very clear that they do NOT know how to utilize the money they have earned through pre-orders and stupid variety of microtransactions through the in game bundles. At its core it's very simple: Premade queues? Short and a fun game to mess around. Solo? Good luck buddy. This game loves feeding your matchmaking with either low ranks or teammates with the IQ of a celsius temperature in a hot summer over in Ibiza. Terrible netcoding, abuse in comms and overall a more toxic community than anyone could anticipate. Mind you, I play in central EU and have yet to find a single person that's willing to cooperate over voice comms after 500 games. THE WORST ISSUE OF ALL, however, apart from the terrible glitches, buggy mess and awful community, IS THE TEAM THAT MANAGES THIS GAME. Rude staff that become sarcastic AFTER YOU DEMAND YOU GET A NORMAL GAMEPLAY FOR THE 25 BUCKS YOU PAID. They ban people on discord, they curse at them and get cocky MEANWHILE pushing for extra miscrotransaction patches because of course.. getting a quick buck is better than anything. Do NOT be fooled, epic games will get the rights to this game soon and it will be free by next christmas in the vault gifts. Good job Sloclap, ya bunch of French hurt losers.

Тяжело смотреть на игру которая с каждым днём уходит куда то не туда. На этапе запуска игры, она давала невероятный опыт соревновательного футбола. Но очень грустно когда игра сталкивается просто с невозможно большим количеством багов, лагов, неприятных пингов и патчи выходят а ничего не меняется.
Как было в пословице - "Куй железо пока горячо", в нашем случае железо уже давно остыло, но разработчик максимально пытается снова разогреть меха. 
Последние патчи и вовсе заставили бросить игру до хороших времен. Управление в игре (в моем случае клавиатура + мышь) достаточно удобное, если бы оно не менялось автоматически ПОСЛЕ КАЖДОГО БЛИН ПАТЧА.
Серьёзно, я надеюсь что только я столкнулся с этой проблемой, когда я зашёл в игру с долгим перерывом чтобы просто поиграть в неё как раньше, а у меня то управление стоит не то, то wasd не работает. 
Добавьте к этому что в игру нужно играть с пингом не выше 80, иначе вы сами того не заметите как сломаете свое устройство швырнув его в стену, либо максимально быстрыми телодвижениями нажмёте на кнопку "Удалить с устройства". 

А теперь поговорим про игроков, тут как бывает и у других онлайн игр есть большая доля нытиков и эмоциональных тюбиков, которые вместо того чтобы просто доиграть пускай и проигранную игру, уходят из игры, выходят из ворот, просто спамят в чате - отлично, и такое происходит уже после 1-го гола. Зачем вы тогда играете если вы ноете после каждого забитого гола по любому поводу? Комьюнити игры (не всё, но многая его часть) чисто завязана на понтах мол, "смотрите я лучший дриблер всея СНГ" и уже выкладывает в тиктоке свои невероятные мувики с шариком. А ещё дают эти странные названия каждому новому движению которое якобы не было задумано игрой, типа : оооо, я сделал рывок туда сюда, пусть будет BOGDAN DASH😎, и с этого ловишь такой кринж что стыдно становится за то что они спорят друг с другом кто быстрее дал название этому движению. 
Онлайн игроков на время сентября 2025 оставляет желать лучшего, система репортов и жалоб отсутствует (по крайней мере на данный момент) благодаря чему ты можешь только стерпеть как твой друг руинит вашу рейтинговую игру, а выходя из матча тебе пишет что ты получишь какой то штраф, но штрафа никакого ты не получишь, так что руинить игры можно легко, просто, и кому угодно.
Да, казалось бы, ты играешь на высоких рангах типа Мастер или Элита и тебе должны попадаться такие же игроки ну или хотя бы 1 звание ниже, которые будут играть серьёзно и не допускать максимально грубых ошибок, но система рейтинговых игр здесь работает таким образом, что в одной команде может быть как в том меме про сравнение друзей парней и девушек (где у парней друзья кто-то выше, кто-то ниже, кто-то толще, кто-то уже), так и здесь имея самый высокий ранг тебе могут спокойно закинуть в команду бронзу или вообще человека без ранга, особенно часто это бывает если онлайн у игры маленький.
Разработчики начали вместо патчей сразу выпускать скины "легендарных игроков" футбольного мира, и не считая что сама игра стоит не так уж и мало (да, я покупал элитное издание) так и тебе в конце концов придётся в неё ещё заливать денюжки если хочешь получить новую косметику и другое. Микротранзакции в чистом проявлении.
Но это просто лирика которая не особо относится к самой игре. 

Оставляю игру до лучших времен и надеюсь, что когда нибудь в неё также будет приятно играть , как было в первый месяц её релиза и на бета тесте.

Game has its issues, but it has been slowly getting better and better. I absolutely love the game, and I believe it will become an incredible game in due time. 
Bugs aside, the game's ranked mode is very strange, but also fun. 
There is no ranked based queue, and you get matchmade with any rank anytime, no organization at all.
You also cannot derank, so players get trapped in ranks where they don't belong. Not that it has any effect on their matchmaking...
Even with its issues, climbing ranks is very fun. I've climbed all the way from bronze to Elite this season! Solo queuing isn't as bad as people say. I made it all the way to masters without a team.

Great game that takes real world strategy of soccer and combines it with fun arcade gameplay with friends. Best with a group, matches are usually competitive in ranked and everyone gets a good feel for different roles as matches play on. There are some netcode wobbles here and there, but overall it's a competitive and fun experience that's unlike anything else.

Honestly, I regret buying this game. It was the worst €50 I've spent in a long time. Was it funny at first? Yeah, sure, I had the time of my life like i've never had for months/years with my friends, but wow, lag spikes, bugs (A LOT OF THEM), players quitting almost every other game, weeks since launch, and the fact they haven't fixed the keyboard and mouse passing mechanic... I can't recommend it, and if you look at the Steam charts, it's in decline in player base every day... In a few months it will be dead.

Maybe they'll revive it in the future, but in my opinion, it's more of a free to play experience than a paid one. They should have listen the players.

I'd love my €50 back, that's my final opinion xd

TL;DR The game is a mess and needs a lot of work, not worth buying at the moment because it feels like it's still in beta.

1) Servers: They are atrocious. Like 6 out of 10 games either I will lag or my friend will lag. All you will see are players teleporting and the ball phasing in and out of reality. 

2) Matchmaking: MM in this game has probably been the most infuriating thing I have seen especially in a ranked match. You are forced to play 5v5 in ranked which basically means you will see more egoists in your team thinking they are the in-game Lamine Yamal. Not only that, you also have to account for the fact either your teammates or your enemies will be lagging like mentioned above. So you will be dragged down by toxic teammates and then as a double whammy you will have an enemy hopping around like a shinigami from Bleach.

3) Controls: This is an odd one. I'm not sure if it's a server thing or controls having a weird delay at times. When you sprint and tackle in this game it results in a slide. But for whatever reason your can stop sprinting to do a normal tackle but the game doesn't register that you stopped running, so it results in a slide. That can cost you a goal due to the loss of speed and recovery time. That is just one of many examples.

A once goldmine that is quickly being ruined after every consecutive patch. What felt like a high reflex action game, which is what Sloclap is known for with its previous games, is being turned into a slog fest patch after patch, catering to the worst of the worst. The DNA of a fast paced arcade game that was once talked about having similarities to fighting games is no longer there. Everything that drew people in at release is slowly being removed. A frustratingly disappointing direction for a game I once praised as one of the best games to ever release. Not recommended in its current state. Over 400 hrs on Game Pass.

Also ganz ehrlich, Rematch ist eine absolute Katastrophe. Ich habe selten ein Spiel gespielt, das so unfertig und verbuggt auf den Markt geworfen wurde. Schon im Hauptmenü merkt man, dass hier etwas ganz gewaltig schiefgelaufen ist – Lags, fehlerhafte UI, und manchmal reagiert das Spiel überhaupt nicht mehr.

Im eigentlichen Gameplay wird’s dann richtig schlimm: Gegner glitchen durch Wände, die Steuerung ist träge und unpräzise, Animationen brechen ständig ab, und manche Spielmodi funktionieren schlichtweg nicht. Ich habe mehr Zeit damit verbracht, Bugs zu dokumentieren, als das Spiel tatsächlich zu spielen.

Und als ob das nicht schon reicht, stürzt das Spiel regelmäßig ab. Speicherstände werden manchmal einfach gelöscht oder nicht korrekt geladen. Für ein Spiel, das Geld kostet, ist das schlichtweg eine Frechheit.

Entwickler: Wenn ihr schon ein Spiel veröffentlicht, sorgt wenigstens dafür, dass es halbwegs spielbar ist. So etwas gehört in die Alpha-Phase – aber sicher nicht in einen bezahlten Release.

Fazit: Spart euch euer Geld und eure Nerven. Rematch ist derzeit ein schlechter Witz, der niemanden zum Lachen bringt.

Действительно интересный и аутентичный геймплей, среди всех игр про футбольчик эта игра самая интересная и захватывающая. Можно часами регать игры одну за другой, особенно если играешь не один, а с друзьями.

Самый огромный, жирный и главный минус этой игры - сетевой код. Мяч просто летает назад - вперед у противника. И это не заслуга его дриблинга: игра просто не понимает, где отрисовать мяч и телепортирует его то за противника, то далеко вперед, а ты уже потратил всю стамину... Сетевой код настолько дырявый, что у себя на экране ты можешь за вратаря взять мяч, пройдет 1 секунда, а потом игра решит, что гол все-таки был. И это не при пинге 200-300.

Game's clearly unfinished. Ranked system and queue needs to be addressed. There are still visual bugs and bugs regarding to gameplay, such as not being able to control the ball with your player occurring in several occasions during my time playing the game. It still needs to be polished in many parts. For example there is no "accept" or "decline" option when finding a match which could address the problem when someone doesn't join the game (for example when we have 9/10 players in game's lobby) leading to waiting until the server kicks you out of the lobby so you can try to queue up again. Teams also feel unbalanced and playing with randoms leads to frustrating gameplay since there is no coordination at all, although this is not entirely devs fault. And also the game as of now feels a bit empty and soulless and needs for stuff to be able to do outside the pitch. The only reason I would recommend this game is if you have other 2-4 friends to play the game with (teams of 5 is the most fun imo) it makes it FAR more enjoyable and fun to spend time on.

Glad to say the new season has made some improvements, but there are still far too many bugs for me to recommend this game at $40 (for example, sometimes you’re forced to sprint in a straight line at random). The addition of 3v3 ranked is a step in the right direction, but it gets repetitive quickly. After just a handful of games, it’s clear nobody wants to be goalie—so most goals come from midfield shots because the goalie abandons their box to take a shot and never returns. Something really needs to be done about the goalie role, whether that’s introducing an AI option or making the position more rewarding. Right now, standing still for six minutes to maybe make one or two saves is just not fun, and it shows.

Como buen amante al Fútbol que soy, Rematch ha sido un videojuego que me ha encantado de principio a fin, el juego termina siendo muy divertido, especialmente si lo juegas con amigos, incluso diría que han conseguido que gente que no le gusta el fútbol le acabe dando una oportunidad, su gameplay al estilo puramente urbano muy del rollo de FIFA Street mezclado con parte de la esencia de Rocket League ha hecho que me enganche durante bastantes horas, su apartado a nivel visual, me gusta mucho, el hecho de que con cada gol que marques se cambie el campo de fútbol entero hace ver al juego muy dinámico y muy variado durante las partidas y eso me mola mucho, los controles, aunque de primeras son algo complicados de aprender, te acabas haciendo a ellos a base de seguir jugando, pero pese a todo lo bueno, el juego tiene muchísimo margen de mejora en cuanto a contenido que traer, se echa en falta especialmente más modos de juego e incluso, más cosméticos para poder personalizar más a tu personaje, espero que sigan mejorando y trayendo más cosas con el paso del tiempo y mejoren con más contenido sus pases de temporada, con respecto a su multijugador, ha mejorado muchísimo en cuanto a fluidez y bugs con el balón, haciendo del juego en la actualidad un espectáculo, pero quiero recalcar, que el juego tiene una comunidad muy tóxica, incluso en el modo competitivo, tras haber llegado a Élite (El rango más alto de todos) me he visto afectado por comportamientos nefastos incluso en rangos altos, hay muy poco compañerismo y trabajo en equipo a la hora de pasar el balón, se nota mucho cuando tu compañero de equipo está jugando con su amigo de turno, porque solo busca pasársela a él todo el tiempo, perjudicando no solo al equipo entero, sino a la experiencia individual de cada jugador, les parece dar igual si eso significa perder el partido en pleno competitivo y eso sin contar que a veces hay muchos Trolls que solo buscan fastidiar el partido a los demás marcando goles en propia, es por esta razón por la que, no recomiendo jugar a este juego estando solo, al menos, durante mucho tiempo, mínimo búscate a un amigo para jugarlo o tu experiencia se verá gravemente afectada en muchas ocasiones, aunque, recalco, que esto no es culpa del juego, sino de los mismos jugadores, yo por mi parte lo seguiré jugando, aunque ya de manera más ocasional, ya que cada partido que te echas no te roba mucho tiempo, pero recalco de nuevo en que, tienen que meter más contenido con mayor frecuencia, especialmente si quieren mantener vivo el juego durante muchos años, dicho todo esto, juegazo, ¡Que viva el fútbol!

Не играйте в это дерьмо. 
Играл в этот кал с релиза, наблюдал за каждым ужасным решением разрабов этой "игры" и могу с уверенностью сказать, что последние стараются добить игру всеми возможными способами. После патча 3 играть стало невозможно от слова совсем, урезали механики которые игроки придумывали последние несколько месяцев, персонаж перестал отзываться на управление регулярно(во время использования ускорения он вообще не может менять направление почему-то), окно действий вообще живет своей жизнью, при том этот "патч" принес больше багов, чем уже было, нет код всё так же оставляет желать лучшего(хотя и стало чуть лучше, проблемы возникают до сих пор). Матчмейкинг вообще рандомный, даже трогать его не хочется, на Мастере 2 дивизиона играют чуваки так же, как они играли в бронзе, новым игрокам в это играть вообще нереально будет.

Uma dica pra quem faz cocô em shopping: Qualquer shopping que tenha 3 andares, o melhor banheiro é o do segundo andar.
Por que o primeiro andar, sempre chega quem está desesperado, mija fora do vaso e etc…
No terceiro, geralmente está a praça de alimentação, aonde o uso é frequente. Já no segundo andar, é onde está aquelas lojas que você não consegue pagar os valores dos produtos.
Ninguém vai nas lojas caras e pensa: ♥♥♥♥♥ preciso CAGAR agora!” Portanto, o segundo andar é o canal.
Outra coisa, ou melhor… dica extra: Se o shopping não tiver 3 andares e tiver banheiros perto das lojas caras, o efeito é o mesmo.

I decided to purchase this game after playing the open, after a brief intro and several games as solo queue I quickly realised in this game you really do need team mates. Having met some great people along the way and forming a small group between us we've managed support each other to climb the ranks and I'd managed to hit Elite. There are some inconsistencies with the game but for all its worth finding a good bunch of players along the way and having fun has been the best part of the game for me. In short, playing solo can be really frustrating, but playing as a group is rewarding. A lot of work still needed on this game and it does feel early access still, but overall It's a fun game!

It's been almost 3 months and this game is getting worse and worse, while still having inexcusable bugs in the game.

- The movement is completely ruined since the last patch, changing direction feels like rotating a truck. Not to mention the skill ceiling is WAY lower than what it was on release. In high end games you are forced into a playstyle of either spamming two moves (ippy slide and dribble forward) or shooting a missile and praying your teammate will score on a 50-50 volley.
- "Missile" meta is even worse than before, it's the easiest and most boring way to score goals. Funnily enough, it's way worse since the patch the developers apparently "addressed it". At least before then you could get priority as a defender by pressing lob 80% of the time. Now it feels like you get priority 10% of the time.
- Matchmaking is HORRIBLE. You almost never play against players your own rank. Queueing solo as elite will get you gold or platinum teammates most of the time. 
- Bugs since day 1: Random disconnects (from both steam and rematch servers), Not being able to hold the ball (100% of the time the ball passes through you).
- Battle pass: I don't even care about battle passes but this one is HORRIBLE. You can see my playtime and I am still not able to unlock the final reward for the season. Keep in mind this is a PAID GAME.

The devs unfortunately have no clue what to do with this game and keep ruining it with horrible patches. Every new patch introduces game breaking bugs, and it overall feels like an ALPHA version of the game. REMATCH was in it's best state the first few weeks (if you ignore the bugs and crashes). It's a real shame since this game such a good foundation and a lot of potential.

Please revert patch 3. I BEG YOU SLOCLAP!!! I WILL BUY ALL THE BATTLEPASSES I PROMISE, JUST REVERT THE CHANGES!!!!!!!!!

Post patch 3 launch I have uninstalled the game and will not be returning. In a beta for the new changes, the devs asked for feedback. Here is what I wrote:

The new changes to the game are extremely unenjoyable. I feel like I have considerably less agency over my character's movement resulting in the previously huge potential for skill expression being eradicated. If you guys release a separate branch of the game that runs on the pre-patch 3 code, I would be willing to pay $60 for it. Feel free to consider this a legally binding statement: "I will pay full AAA price for pre-patch 3 Rematch". In fact, I would pay a monthly subscription fee just to have access to pre-third patch Rematch, even with its buggy player interactions and sub-optimal server tick rate. As the game stands right now, I have uninstalled it and do not plan on returning. Rematch was my favorite game of all time and nothing else played like it. The changes to the movement have ruined the experience for me, and all of my friends have the same opinion. The new mechanics have completely alienated the game's core player-base in favor of less dedicated casual players. I am extremely disappointed in the direction that the devs have decided to move in, and I pray that they come to their senses before too much time has passed and a return to peak form is no longer an option.

Unfortunately this message will likely fall on deaf ears. I feel truly heartbroken.

I'm gonna start off by saying; I really enjoyed this game, I learned the mechanics, practiced almost everyday, to the point where my Youtube Shorts kept teaching me new tech and tactics. But here's the thing; This game has really weird servers. Even though you are in the server you are supposed to be, the ping will always be different, either really good or you get lag spikes that will literally cost you one of the easiest catches of your life. Not only that but the time it takes for updates really hurts this game, things like 3v3 comp and 4v4 comp are literally NEEDED since it just leads people to be either way more skilled than you in either of those modes or you're going against people that clearly are still learning. The best matches are the ones where it's neck and neck, the hype is so there whenever you hit a shot after a tense set-up for it, but because of how the matchmaking is, it has honestly become rare to even find those super tense games anymore. I love this game, I really hope the best for it, they just really need to get it together right now cause the potential is there for sure.

J'aurais pourtant aimé le recommander, mais plus on avance, plus une espèce de rigidité, et d'inertie ou autre mécaniques farfelues apparaissent et prennent le dessus.

Certaines mécaniques étaient juste compliquées, mais pouvaient encore être contrées malgré certains problèmes de lag etc, car on n'avait pas une espèce d'inertie infernale en défense en posture.

Maintenant le simple fait d'avancer puis faire demi-tour, faire 90° sur le côté, c'est semblable à faire tourner un véhicule.
On glisse quand on avance et qu'on met la posture, au point de dépasser le joueur et si jamais il fait certains dribbles, non seulement ça vous passe au travers, mais la balle aussi, pendant que vous continuez de glisser.

Terminé, il est passé d'au moins 10m et inutile de penser rattraper le joueur, c'est terminé.

Donc je laisserai mon avis ainsi, jusqu'à ce que celui-ci soit enfin stable mécaniquement, car c'est infernal.

A cela je rajoute maintenant que la moitié des interactions de tacles sont des délires tels que nous ne savons même plus à 100% si on a taclé la balle ou non, que votre manette vibre, que l'audio fonctionne ou quoique ce soit, juste, nous ne savons pas.

Игра с огромным потенциалом, который патч за патчем просирается в глубокую канаву где лежат сами знаете кто, и сами знаете кого. 

Шанс что все станет лучше чем сейчас, определенно есть, но для этого как минимум нужно делать игру бесплатной, т.к. в текущих реалиях эта игру и за даром не факт что кому-то нужна будет. 

Конечно есть и хорошие моменты, но то что есть хорошее в новых патчах, перекрывает все плохое, и ожидания каждый раз все меньше и меньше. Молчу о том, что разработчики осознанно портят уникальность игры, замедляя её, урезая уникальные механики т.п.
this game stresses me out, but in a good way

-Pros: El Juego es una pasada, el estilo artístico es muy bonito y la jugabilidad está bien puesta junto con las animaciones a la hora de ejecutarlas, cabe recalcar que es más apasionante si lo juegas con amigos y ya dispone de crossplay.
-Contras: Deben mejorar el sistema de prioridad a la hora de los enfrentamientos, tanto de jugador como de portero y balones que van donde no deberían en algunos casos, la toxicidad de los jugadores a la hora de marcarse en propia te mina la moral así que reza para que no te toque este tipo de gente, crasheos a la hora de abrir el steam overlay y chatear con amigos o al iniciar el mismo juego puntualmente ocurren, supongo que con los siguientes parches esto mejorará.

Conclusión: Si quieres divertirte con tu grupo de amigos en un juego de fútbol arcade con temática cartoon, este es tu juego, te esperán horas de vicio, risas y enfados, solamente aprende bien los controles, mejora tus skills, y disfruta.

Rematch est critiqué pour être une sorte de jeu pas fini.
Alors oui, il y a quelques bugs par ci par là, un matchmaking lancé est impossible à interrompre, petits bugs dans le menu...
Honnêtement, le jeu tourne bien.

Au niveau du gameplay, il faudrait ajuster deux trois trucs comme les interceptions (surtout aériennes).

Aussi, les dribbles sont parfois cheatés au point d'être impossibles à arrêter, je pense surtout aux sombreros et aux jongles.

Fondamentalement le jeu est bon, simple et amusant.

En revanche, c'est là qu'il faut bien mettre l'accent : la communauté est à chier.

En effet, vous allez tomber sur une bande de nullards qui campent à coté des cages en attendant que le ballon leur arrive dans les pattes. Ils sont tellement nuls à chier que même en mettant 3-4 buts, si vous jouez en passe vous aurez un meilleur score que ces noobs.
Encore faudrait il que ça marche, ils n'en marquent pas.

Bref, ces trouducs de 8 ans, disséminés aux 4 coins du globe vont vous pourrir la vie, vous allez tenter de jouer correctement et eux vont juste tenter de réceptionner une dégagement.

Grosso modo, si vous jouez tout seul en 3V3 et que vous avec un minimum de tactique, vous allez regarder votre gardien de but faire des dégagements au campeur qui attend dans la surface du gardien adverse.

Le 5V5 classé, quant à lui, doit être classé comme cancérigène. 
C'est un joyeux bordel où les idiots du village de l'Europe Occidentale se retrouvent pour tenter de prendre la baballe et marquer.

Votre équipe sera donc un troupeau qui se piétinera pour la balle, sans communication, sans réflexion, sans talent.

Après quelques heures de jeu, je constate donc que le 3V3 en match rapide avec des amis, est plus plaisant que le compétitif. Vous pourrez jouer ensemble sur discord et vous accorder. Vous allez gagner, perdre, apprendre et établir des stratégies.

En 5V5 compétitif, vous allez uniquement supporter les deux débiles qui ne font pas de passe avec vous et envoient les ballons à peu près partout sauf dans vos pieds ou les cages adverses.

En quasi 12h de jeu, nous n'avons pas gagné une seule game en 5V5 compétitif en classe Bronze la plus basse.

Alors certes, vous avez tout à fait le droit de vous dire que mes amis et moi sommes juste une bande de quiche qui ont des sabots de sanglier en guise de pieds et des pinces de crabes à la place des mains. Mais le fond c'est que le jeu et le matchmaking ne sont pas pensés pour faire de Rematch un bon jeu.

Il y a plein de données sur les parties, on voit très vite qui sont les joueurs solo et ceux qui ne font aucune interception pour défendre.. Bref les gens qui sont foncièrement inutiles.

Ca me semblerait plus marrant de faire un trie avec du matchmaking qui met en relation les joueurs perso entre eux et ceux qui jouent collectif entre eux. Mais bon, si on devait bâtir un mur anti cons à chaque fois qu'on en croise un, le monde serait parsemé de murs de briques.

Bref, passez votre chemin, vous éviterez de vous faire du mal psychologiquement

Dies ist eigentlich eine neutrale Rezension, da das Spiel einiges an Spaß machen kann und mittlerweile sichtbare Fortschritte zeigt. Das Potenzial ist weiterhin enorm, und einige zuvor gravierende Probleme wurden verbessert.

Positiv ist, dass der Battlepass inzwischen deutlich schneller freigeschaltet werden kann und Exploits seltener auftreten. Auch die Wartezeiten wurden kürzer, es gibt nun pro Season einen zusätzlichen Spielmodus. Hier kann jedoch noch ordentlich nachgeliefert werden. Auch gibt es inzwischen eine Zeitstrafe, wenn jemand das Spiel frühzeitig verlässt, und sein Team im Stich lässt. Sehr gut!
Insgesamt fühlt sich das Spielerlebnis dadurch deutlich stabiler an.

Allerdings bleiben ein paar der alten Baustellen bestehen: 
- Die Moderation ist quasi nicht vorhanden, und toxisches Verhalten bleibt damit ein Problem. 
- Das Liga-System wirkt nach wie vor unausgereift. Durch das Mischen der Ligen, kommt es häufig dazu, dass man mit Bronze Spielern gegen Elite spielen muss. Dies ist nicht für die Bronze Spieler gerecht, und als besserer Spieler sehr nervig.
- Egoismus von Spielern wird nicht bestraft. Es gibt weiterhin sehr viele Egoisten, die lieber für sich spielen, anstatt als Team zu agieren, was gerade für den Aufstieg in höheren Ränge frustriert.

Vom Umfang her bleibt es ein recht schlankes Spiel mit den bekannten 3v3-, 4v4- und 5v5-Modi. Der zusätzliche Modus pro Season ist ein Anfang, aber es braucht langfristig mehr Abwechslung und echte Neuerungen, um Spieler zu halten.

Tl;dr: Das Spiel hat sich verbessert und entwickelt sich in die richtige Richtung. Es macht Spaß. 
Wer aber ein wirklich ausgereiftes PvP-Erlebnis sucht, der sollte noch ein Jahr oder mehr warten.

At first, the game was great. It just needed some improvements—maybe some fun activities added—and that’s it.
Instead, the game now feels sluggish, like you wanted to make it more realistic but ended up making it too realistic. Almost overly realistic—even compared to the real world.

Some issues that should have been fixed are still present. A few less important things were changed or fixed and then reverted (like walking speed). Of course many bugs were fixed and thanks for that. There are not cheaters (I think).
In my opinion the game used to be excellent. I think it will be hard to get back to that again. That’s why the game was bought in the first place. Beta testers accepted the game as it was (aside from bugs), and then many things got changed. 

Hope it will be good again.

Edit:

Congratulations. We've got new Ronaldinho skin and dance.

But game? Now Rankeds.

If you stuck at one rank then you're there stuck forever no matter what you do. (If you play without team mates). You can score a lot of goals, make a lot of assists and you lose the same amount of Rank Points as the person who shoots own goals and vice versa.
Player levels are probably still random and someone who plays better is stuck with worse players.
Also no player kicking option if someone is sabotaging their own team.

I love it, I've played a lot of it - I just can't recommend it right now without SBMM (or any real MM for ranked play) and the jankiness around priority.

This game had to be game of the year for me until the latest update. 

Most of my friends wouldn't buy the game because they were worried that it would be to slow, like FIFA. I told them that it wasn't and it was the best game on the market, after a while I was able to get them to join. It had to be the most fun that I have had in a competitive online game in over a decade, I am not joking. This game had me playing everyday for multiple hours, staying up late with friends and even making new friends with the discord servers etc. After all that fun, I refuse to play it. 

It was a great game that had some realistic aspects mixed with fun mechanics that made it really feel like a high speed, high skill ceiling video game. However in the latest patch, they RUINED THE GAME!!! Before the latest patch you had the chance to really show off skills and get around defenders because of the skills that you learned from playing. Post update they made it so now you break the absolute Jesus of the ankles of the defender but somehow it doesn't matter, he will catch up to you no matter how far you are from him. 

The game had a very high skill ceiling, however now with the update I have people just SPAAAAAAMMMMMING the tackle button which used to punish them as they would then have to catch up to the attacker, but due to the lack of speed I have as an attacker after the update there is absolutely no punishment for it. Literally as an attacker you have no way to score apart from passing, but the best part is you can't get around the defender to pass because they are now absolutely broken.

The developers listened to their community and made lots of good changes however, they have successfully driven the game straight into the ground so much so that as the highest rank in the game me and all of my friends who all have 200+ hours in the game have quit. There is no more fun to be taken from this game after the patch. 

This game was so much better then FIFA because it was actually fun, it wasn't just pass the ball all the way up the field or suffer, there was actually skill required for this game. It was high paced, realistic to an effect with fun mechanics that made it more then just a passing simulator with the ability to press another button to shoot, it had ways to really be an all around player with multiple skills that included good aim, passes, dribbling, and much more. With the new update the only skill you can really have is defense, after that its almost just pure luck if you can score because when you have the whole team spamming tackle 24/7 there is almost no way to get around them. 

After 200+ hours in the game I can tell you that 1 HOUR IS ALL IT TOOK TO REALIZE ITS TRASH NOW!!! Before the patch it was buggy as hell and had really, really crap servers but, the reason why people played it was the fact that it was the most fun game on the market for a period of time. Since the update, it is now just a completely buggy mess with terrible game mechanics and no enjoyment to bring to you. 

Please fix your game, I don't want to leave it but I refuse to play it the way that it is right now. YOU TOOK ALL THE FUN OUT OF IT!!!

UPDATE #1 11/5/25

After the last couple of updates I have been pleased to find that the game has become playable again. While it isn't the way it used to be I can now say that I still have fun playing it. I would have to follow that up though with the fact that I still would not recommend this game. 

The game is still extremely buggy sometimes kicking you in the middle of your ranked games, sometimes if gives you bugs where you'll be in the game but unable to do anything but move, this includes not touching the ball, shooting the ball, passing the ball, etc. 

Another thing about the game that really needs to be fixed is the lobby situations. To put into perspective I am the highest rank in the game but for casual games I find that I am in a lobby with people that just started playing the game that same day. I being the highest rank expect that my teammates will have the ability to do most things because I am not used to these newbies that will shoot the ball from half field because why the hell not. The worst part about it is that it's not just casual lobbies, it's also a problem in ranked situations. 

I have found myself on multiple occasions recently where I have been pinned up against teams of players that are the second worst rank in the game, usually I wouldn't care because its just a free way to increase my rank but this is just straight up unfair. Me and my elite friends put up against 3 silver players??? How does that even happen in a ranked lobby?? My friends and I destroyed that team whilst not even trying to and did it within a minute later to find out what their ranks were. 

Finally, Rematch still doesn't have a written chat function. While I could understand how that might not be great, it would still be nice to have, especially considering the quality of their voice chat. There is no way to do push to talk in this game and if I'm not mistaken there is also no way to alter the volume of your teammates mics so its just constant open mic and my teammates will sometimes have something important to say in between the heavy breathing sounds into the mic with no way to turn it down. 

I will say it again, please fix your game.

The game does a good job about mixing the fundamentals of soccer (positioning, passing, teamwork, etc.) with arcade physics. There has been a decent amount of updates since release. One shaky part is server-client sync, because it's very important that you're able to see the correct shot angle to save a ball, dribble direction to make tackles, etc. This has been getting better though, and hasn't stopped my enjoyment of the game.

Overall, I would recommend it. A few months after release, it seems to have a steady 10K-peak playerbase on Steam, and now has crossplay capability. The game is definitely in a very playable state and is improving over time.

This feels as close as almost possible to a real action yard football except with shaolin football skills

This game is so close to being fun, yet somehow it manages to be extremely frustrating almost every game. The bugs in this game are endless, and almost all of them impact core gameplay. Bugs where you or your teammates cannot touch the ball, which gets exceptionally bad when the teammate who cannot touch the ball is in the Keeper role, which just means you now lose the game, fortunately some players have found a way to fix this issue by closing and relaunching the game, which unfortunately means that you are still playing a player down for at least half the game, if they bother to rejoin the game. Other bugs do not have fixes such as, a ball magically flying into your net when there was no one touching it. Yet somehow the game manages to have issues beyond bugs such as the sensitivity with passes, where multiple times per game you can see your teammates or yourself try pass the ball, but instead it goes in the exact opposite direction to give the other team a breakaway, which forces you to play a sport built around passing where everyone is scared to pass, leading to dissatisfactory gameplay. Multiple of me and my friends who play this game have also blue screened multiple times, which leads me to believe that this game is the problem and not our graphics cards. Yet, arguable worst of all Slocap, doesn't seem to care what so ever, adding new skins and celebrations every patch while bugs that the community constantly complains about aren't getting fixed. Even issues that to me would seems apparently clear that multiplayer game would need, such as a report button isn't in the game, which means there is no repercussions for cheating or malicious behaviour in general, such as screaming slurs (both of which happened more often than they should).  It seems abundantly clear to me that Sloclap cares more about their microtransaction sales than actually producing a functional game.

So, i felt like this was an appropriate playtime to make my review. This game is really fun. Matches are short but they can drag on for like 20 minutes in some really intense games. There are some problems with the game but the more i play the less bugs i find so i'm glad that developers are really trying to make this game worth the price tag. My friend gifted this game to me so i didn't really run into that price wall issue. 
That said this game has a MASSIVE problem that the developers won't ever be able to fix and that's the players.
It is a really skill based game you get no new abilities except the knowledge of how to play the game but that is not the issue, the more you play the people you play with get better. The main issue is that the players are very toxic. They leave game after one goal, they spam emotes you can't mute. But if devs add in a report button the game will die cause of the low playerbase.
Even after all that i still think this game is really good. The best way i can think of to fix that issue is to either lower the price or put the game on a sale every two months or so. 
I encourage everybody to try this game cause it may be one of the best team sports games out there. I hope they take this format and release same system for different sports.

Edit: Post Season 1 patch they sped the game back up, so most of this has been fixed. Rematch is damn good fun with a group of 3 or more, and this patch restored the movement back to what it was. The 50/50s are still fairly abundant but it is a minor issue compared to moving your character.

To preface this, I've played this game a good amount with friends and have climbed up to master. This game was really good fun until this most recent patch. There was a lot of fluidity in movement, both on offense and defense, creating a deep mixup game when trying to defend someone dribbling downfield. There was a decently high skill ceiling with learning offensive dribbling and shooting techniques (ippy slide, chops, ee usage, blade kick etc.) and the defense had to learn how to deal with these techniques, which all had answers. The only real issue I had were the 50/50 balls in the box before this patch.
Post patch 3 however, most of this fluidity and mixup game is gone. The movement has become sluggish and unresponsive, just moving your character around feels like turning a barge now. The dribbling feels next to useless to use because you have no movement to follow up your evasion. The skill ceiling has been lowered considerably as a result, basically being forced to pass the ball whenever you are challenged as an offender in any way.
I just don't understand why Sloclap would balance the game this way when most of the players still playing are higher skilled. I'm probably going to put the game down for good if the movement stays the same.

From the moment I launched the game, it was clear that the developers poured both technical skill and creative passion into their work. The presentation is refined, with a clean user interface and an aesthetic that immediately immerses you in its world. Performance is smooth, with minimal loading interruptions and a stable frame rate that lets the experience shine without distraction.

Gameplay strikes a strong balance between accessibility and depth. Newcomers can pick it up quickly, yet there are enough layers of strategy and progression to keep dedicated players engaged long-term. Each mechanic feels purposeful, and the learning curve is rewarding rather than punishing.

Visually, the art direction stands out. Rather than chasing hyper-realism, it embraces a style that feels distinct and memorable. Audio design complements the visuals seamlessly, with a soundtrack and effects that enhance immersion rather than overwhelm it.

But perhaps the strongest quality is its sense of identity. This is not a game that feels derivative; it knows what it wants to be and executes on that vision with confidence. For anyone seeking a polished, thoughtfully crafted experience, this game is easy to recommend.

Verdict: ★★★★☆ — A standout release that deserves a place in your library.

Bought the game to play with friends. It started out fine enough, just the thought that once I learned the controls the game would become fun to play. Learn the controls. Go into a game where I encounter the glitch that happens EXTREMELY frequently where you just can't interact with the ball. You can't have possession of the ball (literally the entire game) all you can do is run around and hope that you get close enough to shoot somewhere. It's an absolute nightmare for new players and this is all without mentioning the ippy slide... That was before Patch 3. 

After Patch 3, we hopped on hoping for some bugging yet still fun gameplay. First game, one of my friends computers blue screens and they have to reset their entire PC so we're down a player for the rest of the game. Next game, the fps drops to the point it looks like a stop motion animation (unplayable, can't follow the ball or anything) and the game is now super laggy to the point where I will intercept the ball and carry it down the field before it disappears and goes to someone else randomly. Our final game to end the night ends with my PC blue screening which is enough to make me uninstall the game. 

It sucks as Rematch is a fun game to play when it isn't crashing or buggy (despite the multiple exploits). But, either the devs don't care or don't play their game because Patch 3 is such an incredible disappointment. They keep saying that their fixing all the bugs but it seems all they're doing is adding more bugs and exploits. Unplayable at this point, I don't know if I'll ever reinstall even if they fix the game. Honestly, at this point, I'd rather reinstall Destiny 2 and farm raids for 6 hours than play Rematch for 1.

SE CHUVEIRINHO NA ÁREA FOSSE "JOGAR BONITO", COMO ESSE JOGO VENDE, O RONNIE SERIA O RONALDINHO.

O jogo é divertido, muito divertido, não é atoa que joguei 58 horas da minha vida nessa bomba. Todavia, o jogo é lotado de bugs e com um meta claro e muito muito chato. 

Os bugs de gameplay são desde bugs visuais, jogadores desaparecem e reaparecem na sua tela, perdem cabeça, a bola some e reaparece, até bugs da dinâmica do jogo em si com dribles fáceis pela latência do jogo e que são indefensáveis, bem como o sistema de atração e prioridade com a bola que são, na melhor hipótese, um RNG disfarçado, isso merece um tópico a parte.

O sistema de atração é aquele que faz o jogador se juntar a bola e ele ter o domínio, ou executar a ação selecionado, por exemplo a bola está na minha frente, mas não no meu domínio, aparece um losango na bola e eu resolvo chutar, meu personagem vai para perto da bola e a chuta. O problema que esse sistema faz, muitas das vezes, o jogador simplesmente se teletransportar para a bola, o que dificulta a vida do marcador e os jogadores constantemente abusam desse sistema para driblar, o que é frustrante, mas não é culpa do jogador, é culpa do desenvolvedor que não retira esse bug do jogo. O sistema de prioridade é a segunda grande decepção, pois não é claro, muitas das vezes a bola está no ar em disputa, múltiplos personagens selecionam uma ação, mas só um a executa e não é possível saber que a fez, bem com a interferência do outro é anulada, isso faz com que o chuveirinho na área seja a melhor estratégia possível, pois eventualmente você terá a prioridade e fará o gol esse é o meta e é chato demais, pois não há drible, nem passe, só chuveirinho.

I wanted to wait a while before I wrote this review, and I feel now is a good time. (writing this as of the launch of patch 3)

Long story short, the core gameplay is fun IF it works. However, I cannot overemphasize the IF.

As a goalie main (I play with the name "goalie only"), my role especially has been plagued with bugs. Ball not going in my hands, players taking it when they shouldn't be able to, etc. When I first started playing, I found the game fun enough to be able to look past these issues, and held faith that Sloclap could fix these issues.

Unfortunately, Sloclap seem to have either made an incredibly broken game that they cannot fix or are simply incompetent as developers. Whichever one it is, the patches have been horrible. Each patch claims to have fixed bugs, which are never fixed, and somehow introduces new bugs every single patch. Patch 3, the most recent patch as of writing this review, was actually pretty good to start. I found the gameplay smoother and more responsive, and I really thought things might be looking up from here. This all ended when I played a ranked game, where the other team lobbed thye ball into the corner, and then magically, while NOBODY was touching the ball, a goal was scored. This is something in my nearly 100 hours goaltending i've never witnessed, which tells me it was somehow introduced in patch 3.

It genuinely boggles my mind how ever single patch for this game somehow makes the game worse. 1 bug "fixed" (still manges to crop up), 6 new, even more frustrating bugs introduced. While I love the core gameplay, I cannot reccomend a game with this many ridiculous bugs, many of which are gamebreaking and have been present since launch. I held faith for a while that it could be fixed, but Sloclap have proven themselves in my eyes to be incredibly incompetent, and lets just say I do not have any desire to play this game anymore. 

TLDR: Fix your game, Sloclap.

This game is not very good. At first you feel like you just need to get a grasp of the controls, and then it will be fun. But even after getting a hold of what button does what, and when to press those buttons, the game still feels absolutely ass. 
Ball control is non-existent. Passing the ball (and therefore creating something remotely similar to the game called football) is nearly impossible. Goalkeeping is just praying the game decides you get to lock onto the ball, only for you to get blocked by three 12 year-olds diving in front of every distribution you try to make. And then, when you finally show up 1v1 vs. the goalkeeper, you shoot..... the ball into the left-side wall of the court because your character suddenly aims at the way his face is looking.
Absolute garbage. Tried it with a friend, we both cried.

[h1] Les problèmes de Rematch menacent son développement/avenir [/h1] 

Rematch possède des problèmes au niveau de la conception bien plus grave à présent que le comportement des joueurs
Précédemment, le problème résidait dans certains joueurs refusant toute coopération, que ça soit en attendant devant les cages adverses, en spammant la touche demandant "La passe", "Centre", "Sur la ligne". Cela peut paraître dorénavant anecdotique face aux problèmes que rencontrent Rematch

De nombreux aspects du gameplay de base sont si DÉFAILLANTS que je me demande parfois si le jeu a été testé par l'équipe de Developpement/QA avant sa sortie. Voici les éléments totalement hallucinants que je rencontre sur ce jeu.

1. La qualité des contrôles : ça m'arrive très souvent de déclencher un tacle glissé par accident alors que je souhaite presser l'adversaire et effectuer plutôt un tacle "debout" (sans glisser). Je conçois que je ne suis pas un joueur pro sur les jeux de sport et sincèrement, la détection de la relache sur la touche de sprint et l'impossibilité de "binder" les deux types de tacles sur des touches séparés est TRES dommageable
Toujours sur les tacles, récupérer le ballon demande souvent plusieurs tentatives, surtout près de la ligne de touche. Une fois sur la ligne ou près du corner, je peux vous assurer que vous en tirer dans spammer les tacles (debout ou glissés) est quasiment impossible et qu'il existe une succession de ces types de tacle
J'ai expériencé aussi un bug très bizarre, où les joueurs ne pouvaient plus toucher le ballon. ça m'est arrivé moi aussi et on se retrouve à attendre et ne pas quitter par respect pour les autres joueurs

2. Le comportement en tant que gardien : arrêter des tirs surpuissants -> aucun problème pour certains gardiens mais ce même gardien rencontre des problèmes pour les tirs/passes les plus lentes qui le transpercent. C'est inconcevable et la vue du replay est risible au possible

3. Le netcode : je pense que tous mes confrères qui ont commenté rematch peuvent vous en parler aussi bien que moi et je souhaite en ajouter une couche en indiquant que ce netcode est médiocre, les bugs, les saccades, les mouvements de balle INEXPLICABLES et tout ce que j'ai pu oublier rendent médiocre ce jeu face à des jeux beaucoup plus anciens. Je pense à son concurrent avec des voitures volantes

Le jeu dans son état actuel ne ressemble pas un jeu de foot arcade ou à un jeu tout court. [u] C'est le chaos le plus total à chaque game [/u] , entre les lags, l'exploitation de technique/bug (comme le "ippy slide" ou autre, toujours pas patché à ce jour) 

Je conçois que pour survivre, le jeu a besoin de contenu "payant" pour financer le développement. [b] Je m'interroge, est-ce vraiment l'urgence [/b] face à l'hémorragie des joueurs qui partent due en grande partie à ces problèmes inhérents à Rematch ? 

Rematch peut être très amusant quand il fonctionne et j'ai apprécié jouer au jeu. Je résumerai ce jeu en "dommage" puisqu'il a énormément de potentiel ruiné par une expérience qui parait déplaisante une fois que l'on remarque que les problèmes ponctuels sont récurrents.

[h2]Short Version > Blue Parts[/h2]

[h2]Great little multiplayer game.[/h2]  Even disregarding that the dev team is comparatively a small one, the game is in really good shape and the mechanics and presentation are already very solid.
[h2]If you're expecting a FIFA/PES clone[/h2] (which you shouldn't be) then you're going to be sorely disappointed. [h2]This game is not that.[/h2]
I'd say it has some elements of games like Rocket League and FIFA Street.
Very much it's own thing, though.

And also partially because of that, [h2]feels like it has a pretty steep learning curve.[/h2] Controls and the camera etc. can also take a while to adjust too. I'd wager most will take a little time before the game even "clicks" with them and again a little more time,before being able to concentrate on improving skill.

[h2]Very much a team game.[/h2] You're fun *very* much depends on the quality of your teammates. Communication is very important here, so having a team of non-communicating randoms compared to a team who know each other and are communicating over voice... makes a very big difference. The in-game solutions like calling for a pass and having the direction shown are decent, but can't make up it.
Oh. I muted the in-game audio chat fairly early on. Too many bad/open mics - as you'd expect from the internet and most games with open voice chat.

Which on the other hand also means that [h2]playing with a bunch of buddies, can be crazy fun.[/h2] To the point where being a coordinated team which can communicate -can actually noticeably make up for an eventual skill/experience difference. More so than for example in Rocket League.
And so the flip-side is, that already 1 or (and not uncommonly) more bad teammates will ruin your games. You do have times playing alone, where you do get lucky enough that the entire rest of the team is at least competent and/or just being dumb. While more rare, those games are what get you hooked and are mad fun and entertaining.
In my experience this game is way more entertaining with friends.
Much less the case, when playing solo. But if the stars do happen to align and your team does play well and things fall in place, the game can be equally as fun and rewarding.

[h2]Not to say there aren't any bugs[/h2] or areas where the game can improve, expand and eventually add more polish and content. While they few and rarely big problems, there are some bugs and some that have been there since at least the beta. I feel though, that the devs are making progress and putting in effort. Time will tell :)


[h2]It's comparatively cheap too. Feels appropriate.[/h2]
In-game currency seems aaalright... kinda hard to say. But you can earn free stuff and the season pass doesn't feel too grindy. At the moment.
It's not even close to the monetization bullcrap, found in FIFA/FC though.
No player stats. Everyone is equal. So the currency is purely for cosmetic stuff. 
It's a matter of taste, but I like the art style generally and it's well executed. 

[h2]The game is fast, direct, arcade-y fun.[/h2] When it flows and things go well for you, it feels awesome!
The visuals are really good, with excellent and satisfying animations. Easy to hop in and out of, too. Games aren't super long and jumping back into a new game doesn't tend to take long.
There are a bunch of things that do need attention though - which is normal for a game of this type. This type of game especially, requires "constant" tweaks and fixes, which, at the moment at least, if feels like it's getting.

But for it being the very first release of this game I actually find it impressive, just how solid this game was on release. Rematch could have been in a lot worse state on release, or call itself an Early Access Game, to legitimise a poor, or incomplete state on release. Which unfortunately, is starting to feel like it's becoming the norm with other, similar games.

[h2]Worth checking out.[/h2]
I have around 250 gours on Playstation and now play on PC too. I love the game 100%. It's not a perfect game but a crazy fun one specially with friends.

Every time I hold out on the hope that they'll fix this game, the more I'm let down. I understand game dev isn't as simple, but more play testing, delaying release until the game's in a better state AND reducing the price of early access would've helped this title greatly. In it's current state the game's FAR from worth it, that's a given...

[h1] Some of the most fun I've had with a multiplayer title in a hot minute. [/h1]
[h2] That being said... [/h2]
This game needs a lot more regular care given to it by the developers. Although I love this game, it's clear that it is not a fully finished product: Some of this is due to basic gameplay mechanics that feel limiting and should definitely be adjusted (goalkeeper controls being way too limited, passing being unreliable, etc.), but most of it is due to technical issues that are objective problems that need to be fixed. I was hoping most of these bugs would gradually get resolved throughout Season 0. But now, a week from its end, I found myself disappointed with how many of these problems, which have been present since day 1, still remain.

Rematch is extremely fun WHEN IT WORKS. When you are able to load into a match, and every player also loads in, and everyone is actually able to move their character (you would think that's the bare minimum), and no one has that bug where they are completely unable to interact with the ball as if they are the ghost of Christmas future haunting the stadium... that's when Rematch becomes one of the most enjoyable 6+ minutes of gaming currently on the market.
Unfortunately, not every match fits this description.

These persistent issues make it very difficult to bring yourself to play Rematch competitively, since the result of a match is highly dependent on if either team has any players that happen to be randomly selected to be cursed with one of the aforementioned bugs. There's also the issue that the competitive rating system is absolutely a placeholder (at least I hope it is) because you gain or lose the same amount of ratings for every game, no matter your performance, the total game score, the rating ratio between your team and the enemy, and even if a player has disconnected or not.

When approached with the entirely casual mindset of "playing cool football game wow rainbow flick so cool!" (and spending way too much time in the character creator), Rematch becomes a very fun game that is surprisingly relaxing to play after a long day. This alone is enough to make this game an easy recommendation for me. However, knowing that it has so much potential to be possibly the best football game on the market, I hope the devs don't settle for the middling success that the game is currently receiving, and keep showing this game the care it needs to fulfill its potential.

Decided to update the review since my last review was right after Patch 3, the worst patch which ruined this game. 

This game has incredible potential and has a really good fundamental core, the closest you can get to "football" as you can compared to FIFA and or PES in my opinion. 

Problem is continuously shoots its self in the foot by Devs trying to make it more closer to football when it's actually Futsal. 

Now I'm not gonna get into the deep details of differences between Futsal and Football, but simply watching a game of football and futsal, you'll notice futsal is fast pace, everyone can dribble, and yes including the goalkeeper, it's very aggressive and an average of 8 goals are scored in a real futsal game.

This game is futsal fundamentally but the devs keep trying to steer it into football by:
Making it slower
Making defending stronger
Nerfing attackers
Buffing goalkeepers

Now the game control wise and feature wise, it isn't deep at all, actually the controls are pretty simple, dribbling can be difficult and complex but honestly it's not that hard, after a while you'll quickly realize this game's skill ceiling is actually determined by intelligence, game sense and football intelligence than actual skill.

So players who know:
When to make a run
When to pass to a player making a run
Moving in a predictable manner for your teammates
Positioning yourself in a good place so you can receive a pass
Man marking a player when out of possession
General patience
etc etc

Now all of this sounds nice and dandy but you'll quickly realize that the majority of this community does not watch football, barely knows anything about football,  their football knowledge comes from FIFA and Bluelock.

So if you want this game, make sure you have friends who also would like to play it with you AND OR you're not someone who's shy about going into Discords and looking for squads to play with.

Otherwise if you play solo, I can guarantee you will not have fun, you will get constantly tilted and will probably rage quit a lot because this game has an ATROCIOUS matchmaking system and griefers / toxic players literally can do whatever they want as the devs will not moderate it at all.

Buy it while on sale because for what it offers it's not worth 25€.

TLDR: Game fun if you watch football and have friends to play with otherwise save your money and state of mind.
Has great potential, but still needs a lot of work
Could be the worst football game I have played.
